question.text: Mahesh sets up a discretionary trust for his three children where the trustee has full discretion on income distribution. What is the tax treatment of income earned by this discretionary trust?

answer.text: Taxed in the hands of each beneficiary at their individual slab rates
Taxed at the Maximum Marginal Rate (MMR) of 30%
Taxed in the hands of the settlor under clubbing provisions at the settlor's slab rate
Tax-exempt since trusts are not separate taxable entities in India

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A discretionary trust where beneficiaries' shares are not determinate is taxed at the Maximum Marginal Rate (MMR) of 30%. In contrast, a determinate trust (where shares are specified) is taxed per Sec 161(1) in the hands of beneficiaries. **The Trap:** Candidates may think all trust income is taxed to beneficiaries or to the settlor. The key distinction is discretionary (MMR) vs determinate (Sec 161(1)). Also note: in a revocable trust, income is taxed to the settlor. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM X-B tests trust taxation. Types include: Public vs Private, Revocable (alternative to will, assets taxed to settlor) vs Irrevocable (Sec 77-78, cannot be amended). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The 30% MMR tax makes discretionary trusts tax-inefficient. If the beneficiaries are in lower tax brackets, a determinate trust with specified shares may be more tax-efficient.
